title: pipeline software-greenfield
purpose: C1.1 defines pipeline software greenfield for the agent-driven expert system. Product execution — pipelines, tasks, program, delivery.
scope: Owns `C1.1` only; siblings under `C1` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §5 — Branch C — Product execution plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#5-branch-c-product-execution-plane).
behavior_steps: At H1 plan approval for a new repo, route-tier or program-scoper sets `pipeline_id` to `software-greenfield` and seeds state.json with the first phase skill as `next_action` (typically spec-parser).

Each pursuit turn runs [A2.1](A2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) preflight then exactly one greenfield phase per [A2.2](A2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md)—spec-parser → hld-writer → dd-writer → diagrams → task-breakdown → scaffold → implement loop—skipping phases the journal already marks complete.
Self-gate and evidence gates apply after H1 unless [J3](J3-strict-hitl.md) strict HITL overrides; implement tasks require verifier evidence before `last_verify=passed` advances the loop.

Consumer goals from [A1.1](A1.1-goal-id-parent-goal-goal-type.md) attach to greenfield phases via task cards and `next_action`; scope completion triggers [A2.4](A2.4-goal-scope-complete-run-goal-verify.md) rather than arbitrary phase skipping.

If `pipeline_id` mismatches repo state, a phase runs out of order, or evidence is missing before advance, pursuit halts at H2 until the conductor dual-writes corrected journal sequencing and state.json routing.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.

acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: C1.1 defines pipeline software greenfield for the agent-driven expert system. Product execution — pipelines, tasks, program, delivery. In practice, the system at h1 plan approval for a new repo, route-tier or program-scoper sets `pipeline_id` to `software-greenfield` and seeds state.json with the first phase skill as `next_action` (typically spec-parser), then each pursuit turn runs [a2.1](a2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) preflight then exactly one greenfield phase per [a2.2](a2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md)—spec-parser → hld-writer → dd-writer → diagrams → task-breakdown → scaffold → implement loop—skipping phases the journal already marks complete.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-c 2026-06-28 -->

Greenfield pipeline is the initial build path: parse spec, HLD, DD, diagrams, task breakdown, scaffold, implement loop, done. Phases run in order unless the journal records prior completion; gates default to self-gate plus evidence after H1 except where strict HITL overrides.

This pipeline_id is the default for new repos without an existing delivery baseline. Pursuit attaches consumer goals to greenfield phases via state.json `next_action` and task cards. See AGENTS.md pipeline section for phase skill mapping.
